figures.caption: Table 4: Overview of major Pharmacovigilance Databases: EudraVigilance, VigiBase, and FAERS

Figure 2: Nonparametric empirical Bayes (general-gamma) signal strength estimation results for the FAERSopioid–mental data that visualizes empirical posterior inferences on top 10 prominent AEs across 5 opioid drugs (color-coded). Points and bars denote posterior medians and 90% equi-tailed credible intervals for signal strength parameter {λij} corresponding to AE-drug pairs. The vertical red dotted line indicates the reference value of “1”. The text on the right reports the observed counts and the expected counts under independence for each AE-drug pair.
Table 1: Accessibility of methods in SRS data mining

Figure 1: Signal detection results on the FAERS-opioid–mental data obtained from pseudo-LRT (panel A), generalgamma (panel B) and GPS (panel C). For each panel, rows correspond to the 10 prominent AEs, and columns correspond to the five opioids. Each cell displays observed report count N , expected null baseline E (B, C) and respective measures of significance for corresponding AE-drug pair.

Figure 4: Nonparametric empirical Bayes (general-gamma) signal strength estimation results for the vigiBaseopioid–mental data that visualizes empirical posterior inferences on top 10 prominent AEs across 5 opioid drugs (color-coded). Points and bars denote posterior medians and 90% equi-tailed credible intervals for signal strength parameter {λij} corresponding to AE-drug pairs. The vertical red dotted line indicates the reference value of “1”. The text on the right reports the observed counts and the expected counts under independence for each AE-drug pair.

Table 3: Collapsed 2× 2 contingency table for AE-i and drug-j, i = 1, . . . , I, j = 1, . . . , J . N•• is the grand total; Ni• and N•j are the i-th row marginal count and j-th column marginal count from the SRS frequency table (see Table 2).

Figure 3: Signal detection results on the VigiBase-opioid–mental data obtained from pseudo-LRT (panel A), generalgamma (panel B) and GPS (panel C). For each panel, rows correspond to the 10 prominent AEs, and columns correspond to the five opioids. Each cell displays observed report count N , expected null baseline E (B, C) and respective measures of significance for corresponding AE-drug pair.
